skill.name: pitchcraft
skill.description: Build persuasive briefings for project kickoffs, status updates, wrap-ups, investor pitches, and solution selling using a five-part Hook, Context, Proposal, Evidence, and Ask framework.
skill.summary: Create audience-focused briefings that turn project facts into clear decisions and support requests.
skill.author: moshuying

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and obfuscation patterns. Review found most command and network hits are markdown examples, templates, URLs, license text, or non-English content, with no evidence of prompt injection or data exfiltration. The main confirmed risk is an npm postinstall installer that writes a skill file into hidden agent directories, so publication should include a filesystem-side-effect warning.

medium_findings.title: Installer Writes to Agent Skill Directories During npm Install
medium_findings.description: The package defines a postinstall script and scripts/install.js copies SKILL.md into .claude, .cursor, or .codex skill directories. This is consistent with the tool purpose, but it creates filesystem side effects during package installation and can modify hidden agent configuration directories without an explicit separate install step.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The postinstall entry and copySkill implementation are direct evidence of filesystem writes. The behavior appears intentional and bounded to skill installation paths, so it is a medium-risk side effect rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Detections Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The Bash and PowerShell patterns appear in install instructions and README examples for creating directories, copying skill files, and verifying installation. They are not dynamically executed by the skill content itself, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced markdown command examples and install documentation. They do not show user-controlled shell execution inside application code.

low_findings.title: Network URL Detections Are Metadata and Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to the repository, bug tracker, star-history badge, Vercel skills CLI, and the Apache license. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in package metadata, README badges, installation references, and license text. I did not find fetch, http client, or network transmission code in the inspected installer.

low_findings.title: Chinese Text Triggered High-Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The high-entropy findings point to Simplified Chinese markdown files. Manual review shows translated documentation and prompt templates, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are readable Chinese markdown. The entropy signal is explained by non-English text and does not indicate obfuscation in context.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Sweep Found No Malicious Override Instructions
low_findings.description: No evidence found of text instructing the evaluator or runtime agent to ignore prior instructions, skip security review, claim pre-approval, or override system authority. The only nearby phrase describes using the prompt as system instructions in Codex CLI documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found normal usage instructions for a persuasion assistant. The wording does not attempt to alter marketplace audit instructions or bypass security controls.

dangerous_patterns.title: npm postinstall Hook With Filesystem Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: package.json runs node scripts/install.js after installation, and the installer creates directories and copies SKILL.md into agent configuration paths. This pattern deserves user disclosure because package installation changes local agent state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The postinstall hook and fs.mkdirSync/fs.copyFileSync calls are explicit. The destination is constrained to known skill directories, which lowers severity.

content.value_statement: Project facts often become activity logs that do not lead to decisions. Pitchcraft turns those facts into audience-focused briefings with a clear ask, evidence, and risk judgment.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through five briefing scenarios: kickoff, status update, wrap-up, investor pitch, and solution selling.
Uses a Hook, Context, Proposal, Evidence, and Ask structure for each output.
Prompts for audience role, familiarity, concerns, metrics, risks, and missing materials.
Provides English and Simplified Chinese prompt and documentation variants.
Includes install guidance for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and general chat workflows.
Runs a pre-submit checklist for sourced metrics, risk response, and audience relevance.
limitations: It does not verify business metrics or market claims independently.
It needs user-supplied facts, audience context, and decision goals to produce accurate output.
It is not a slide design system or presentation renderer.
The npm installer may write files into local agent skill directories.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Leadership Status Update
use_cases.description: Turn progress, risks, and decisions into a concise update for directors, executives, or PMO stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Shape a Product Kickoff Brief
use_cases.description: Frame market need, scope, resources, milestones, and risks so leaders can approve the next phase.

use_cases.title: Draft an Investor or Customer Pitch
use_cases.description: Connect audience pain, product value, proof, and next steps for funding or solution-selling conversations.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Status Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a status update. Ask me for the audience, project goal, completed work, metrics, risks, decisions needed, and next phase.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Kickoff Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pitchcraft to prepare a project kickoff brief. The audience is [role]. Ask for missing background, opportunity, scope, milestones, resources, and risks before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my briefing draft with the Hook, Context, Proposal, Evidence, and Ask framework. Identify weak persuasion, missing evidence, vague risks, and unclear asks.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Between Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this status update into an investor or customer pitch. Preserve accurate facts, change the hook for the new audience, and make the evidence and ask fit the scenario.

output_examples.input: A team needs to brief leaders after a delayed integration milestone.
output_examples.output: The output starts with the key risk and business impact, explains the root cause, compares options, recommends a path, and asks for a specific cross-team decision.

output_examples.input: A founder needs a first investor pitch for an early product.
output_examples.output: The output frames the market pain, describes the solution and current stage, lists early traction or proof, and closes with the funding or partnership ask.

output_examples.input: A product lead has raw notes for a kickoff review.
output_examples.output: The output organizes the notes into opportunity, existing assets, scope, milestones, differentiation, resources, and top risks.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, decision goal, and time limit before asking for a draft.
Include metric definitions, sources, and dates so the briefing can support scrutiny.
State risks with root cause, mitigation, owner, and current status.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to generate unsupported claims or invented market data.
Do not treat the output as final before checking numbers, names, and commitments.
Do not run install commands from the package without understanding the filesystem changes.

faq.question: What kinds of briefings does pitchcraft support?
faq.answer: It supports project kickoffs, status updates, wrap-ups, investor pitches, and solution selling.

faq.question: Does it write weekly activity logs?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on persuasive briefings that explain context, evidence, risks, and a clear ask.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The repository includes Claude Code plugin guidance and prompt files for Codex, Cursor, and chat tools.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes. The repository includes Simplified Chinese README, command, and universal prompt files.

faq.question: Will it verify my data?
faq.answer: No. You must provide and verify sources, dates, metric definitions, and business claims.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The npm package has a postinstall script that can copy skill files into local hidden agent directories.
